response:
nurse emily organized a creative writing workshop for the older adults in her care facility. she knows that writing can be therapeutic and wants to ensure everyone has the opportunity to participate. there are 24 residents interested in joining the workshop. emily decides to divide them into groups, each containing 6 participants, so that each group can have personalized attention and support. after forming the groups, emily plans to allocate 3 writing prompts per group for the session. if each group finishes their prompts and wants to try more, emily has prepared an additional set of 10 extra prompts to be shared equally among all the groups.  

the total number of writing prompts needed is calculated as follows: 24 residents divided into 4 groups, each with 6 participants, gives 4 groups. each group gets 3 initial prompts, so 4 groups × 3 prompts = 12 initial prompts. the extra prompts are 10, to be shared equally among all 24 residents, which is 10 ÷ 24 = 0.4167 per resident. rounding up, emily needs to have 10 prompts for each group.  

the total number of writing prompts is 12 initial prompts + 10 extra prompts = 22 prompts. emily needs to have 22 writing prompts in total to cover both the initial and extra prompts for the session. the library contains 22 prompts, including the initial and extra ones. the library is a source of inspiration for the writing session.  

the library is a key resource for the writing prompts, and it is essential to ensure that the prompts are available for all participants. the library is a central part of the workshop, and it is important to have enough prompts to meet the needs of all participants. the library is a valuable asset that supports the creative process.
